title: Mailchimp vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your business best.
h1: Mailchimp vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Mailchimp and Drip are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and business needs. Mailchimp excels as an accessible, beginner-friendly option with a free tier, while Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with deeper store integration. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right platform for your business goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Freemium (free up to 500 contacts, paid from $13/month)
Drag-drop editor, automation, landing pages, A/B testing
comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You're a beginner or startup with a tight budget and want to start free
You need a general-purpose marketing platform with landing pages and basic automation
You prefer a user-friendly interface and don't require specialized e-commerce features
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need advanced product tracking and revenue attribution
You want SMS marketing alongside email campaigns
You're willing to invest in a premium solution designed specifically for online sellers

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for beginners and small businesses due to its free tier and ease of use. Drip is better for e-commerce businesses that need sophisticated product tracking and revenue attribution. Mailchimp also has stronger Trustpilot reviews, while Drip scores slightly higher on G2.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or Drip?
faq.a: Mailchimp is significantly cheaper, especially if you're just starting out—it offers a free tier for up to 500 contacts. Drip has no free option and starts at $39/month. However, if you have many subscribers, Drip's pricing structure may eventually become competitive depending on your contact volume.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms allow you to export your contact lists and import them into another service. Both Mailchimp and Drip support standard export/import formats. However, you may lose some automation workflows and custom data during the transition, so plan accordingly.
